bullets.text: Q2 companywide CR 91.9 including significant weather activity; Personal Lines CR 88.6, improving 10.9 points YOY on lower frequency and 2023 rate earn-in; personal auto frequency down 8% YOY, severity relatively flat.

Companywide NPW grew 22% YOY; Personal Lines NPW $14.6B (+26% YOY); both Agency and Direct channels posted best-ever Q2 quote and new application volumes; 8 states saw small rate decreases reflecting profitability headroom.

Personal Lines PIFs reached nearly 28 million; personal auto added 1.1 million policies in Q2 alone (2 million YTD); Progressive added an estimated 1% of all 300 million U.S. cars to its book in the first half of 2024.

Property CR 166.3 in Q2 (YTD 130.4); weather losses contributed 76 CR points in Q2 driven by record severe storm activity in May — most active month in 13 years; 15 billion-dollar weather events in H1 2024 vs. the 8.5 annual historical average.

Property rates increased ~4% in Q2 (17% on trailing 12 months); personal auto strategy shifted from rate increases to growth mode with non-rate restrictions unwound in almost all states; focus now on delivering competitive rates.

Debt-to-total capital fell to 22.8%, lowest since February 2022; no bond maturities until 2027; H1 investment portfolio FTE return 1.7%; duration extended to 3.2 years reflecting anticipated Fed rate actions.

Auto model 8.9 in 8 states (16% of PIFs); model 8.8 in 21 states (~50% of PIFs); property 5.0 models elevated to 8 states (~25% of property premium); BOP in 46 states with new multi-product quoting experience for agents piloted.

Commercial Lines CR 88.6, improving 7.8 points YOY; commercial auto competitors continued aggressive rate-taking; for-hire transportation BMT remains pressured by freight market; business auto and contractor BMTs growing.

Commercial auto model 8.2 launched in 2 states; BOP launched in Florida (45th state, 76% of commercial multiperil market); new BOP model in 22 states; combined CL/BOP quoting experience piloted for agents.

Progressive earned Gallup Exceptional Workplace Award for the fourth consecutive year; employee stories highlighted the company's culture of compassion in claims handling and volunteer time-off enabling community impact.
